    <h1 {...{
      "id": "css-styling"
    }}>{`CSS Styling`}</h1>
    <p>{`Style your plugin's user interface using CSS classes, inline styles, or JavaScript`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP supports standard CSS for styling your plugin's interface. You can apply styles using `}<strong parentName="p">{`CSS classes`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`inline styles`}</strong>{`, or `}<strong parentName="p">{`JavaScript`}</strong>{`—choose the approach that fits your workflow best.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"
    }}>{`Prerequisites`}</h2>
    <p>{`Before you begin, make sure your development environment uses the following versions:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest version v5`}</strong>{` or higher`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`If you're new to UXP plugin development or coming from a native extension background, this section provides a quick reference for the three standard approaches to applying styles in UXP. While these methods mirror standard web development practices, it's worth noting how they work in the UXP context before exploring more advanced patterns like theme awareness.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="3" languages="HTML, JavaScript, CSS" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!-- 1. Using CSS classes -->
<div class="green-background">
  <h1>Styled with CSS class</h1>
</div>

<!-- 2. Using inline styles -->
<div style="background-color: yellow;">
  <h1>Styled inline</h1>
</div>

<!-- 3. Using JavaScript -->
<div id="exampleDiv">
  <h1>Styled with JavaScript</h1>
</div>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Apply styles via JavaScript
const exampleDiv = document.getElementById("exampleDiv");
exampleDiv.style.backgroundColor = "orange";
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "stylescss"
    }}>{`styles.css`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-css"
      }}>{`/* Define styles in a stylesheet */
.green-background {
  background-color: green;
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "theme-awareness"
    }}>{`Theme Awareness`}</h2>
    <p>{`Premiere supports theme awareness, which means that your plugin's UI can adapt to the user's chosen application theme—either, `}<strong parentName="p">{`Light`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`Dark`}</strong>{`, or `}<strong parentName="p">{`Darkest`}</strong>{`. In Premiere UXP plugins, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`document`}</inlineCode>{` object implements a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`theme`}</inlineCode>{` property with a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getCurrent()`}</inlineCode>{` method that returns the current theme as a string.`}</p>
    <p>{`Additionally, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`theme`}</inlineCode>{` implements a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`onUpdated`}</inlineCode>{` event listener that is triggered when the user manually changes it. Its callback can then update your plugin's UI with custom CSS classes to match the new theme.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-change-styles-on-the-fly"
    }}>{`Example: Change styles on the fly`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="HTML, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml-1"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<body>
  <h4 id="plugin-heading">Application Info</h4>
  <div class="main-div">
    <div id="plugin-body"> </div>
  </div>
  <footer>
    <button id="btnPopulate">Populate Application Info</button>
    <button id="clear-btn">Clear Application Info</button>
  </footer>
</body>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`function updateTheme(theme) {
  panelBody = document.getElementById("plugin-body");
  panelHeading = document.getElementById("plugin-heading");

  // Change styles based on the new theme on the fly
  if(theme.includes("dark")) {
    panelBody.style.color = "#fff";
    panelHeading.style.color = "#fff";
  } else {
    panelBody.style.color = "#000";
    panelHeading.style.color = "#000";
  }
}

// Listen for theme changes
document.theme.onUpdated.addListener((theme) => {
    updateTheme(theme);
})

// Apply initial theme on load
const currentTheme = document.theme.getCurrent();
updateTheme(currentTheme);
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-switch-theme-classes"
    }}>{`Example: Switch theme Classes`}</h3>
    <p>{`This example demonstrates how to use CSS custom properties (variables) to handle theme changes efficiently. The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updateTheme()`}</inlineCode>{` function applies a single theme class (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`theme-light`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`theme-dark`}</inlineCode>{`) to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`body`}</inlineCode>{` element. When the theme changes, it removes the existing theme class and adds the appropriate one.`}</p>
    <p>{`This approach leverages CSS cascade to update your entire plugin. In `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`styles.css`}</inlineCode>{`, you define CSS variables at the theme level—for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--heading-color`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--body-color`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--border-color`}</inlineCode>{`. These variables are then referenced throughout the stylesheet using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`var(...)`}</inlineCode>{`. When you change the theme class on the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`body`}</inlineCode>{` element, all elements automatically inherit the correct colors without requiring individual updates.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="3" languages="HTML, JavaScript, CSS" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexhtml-2"
    }}>{`index.html`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<body>
  <sp-heading>My Plugin</sp-heading>
  <div class="plugin-body">
    <sp-body>This is the main content area.</sp-body>
  </div>
</body>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-2"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`function updateTheme(theme) {
  // Apply theme to body element only - CSS variables will handle the rest
  document.body.classList.remove("theme-light", "theme-dark");
  document.body.classList.add(
    theme.includes("dark") ? "theme-dark" : "theme-light"
  );
}

// Listen for theme changes
document.theme.onUpdated.addListener((theme) => {
  updateTheme(theme);
});

// Apply initial theme on load
const currentTheme = document.theme.getCurrent(); // Returns "light", "dark", or "darkest"
updateTheme(currentTheme);

`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "stylescss-1"
    }}>{`styles.css`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-css"
      }}>{`/* Theme CSS Variables */
body.theme-light {
  --heading-color: #2c2c2c;
  --body-color: #4a4a4a;
  --border-color: #40548e;
}

body.theme-dark {
  --heading-color: #f5f5f5;
  --body-color: #e0e0e0;
  --border-color: #97ace6;
}

body {
  padding: 0 16px;
}

.plugin-body {
  margin-top: 5px;
  padding: 16px;
  overflow: scroll;
  height: 300px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  border: 2px solid var(--border-color);
}

sp-heading {
  padding: 16px 20px;
  margin: 0 0 12px 0;
  font-size: 18px;
  color: var(--heading-color);
}

sp-body {
  padding: 20px;
  margin: 0;
  line-height: 1.6;
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--body-color);
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "uxp-css-variables"
    }}>{`UXP CSS Variables`}</h3>
    <p>{`Spectrum CSS variables that are `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/photoshop/uxp/2022/guides/theme-awareness/#built-in-theme-colors"
      }}>{`available in other Creative Cloud applications`}</a>{` (for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--uxp-host-text-color`}</inlineCode>{`) are `}<strong parentName="p">{`not yet supported`}</strong>{` in Premiere. They will be added in a future release.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "important-considerations"
    }}>{`Important Considerations`}</h2>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`UXP is not a browser`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP does not support all CSS properties. For example, CSS Grid Layout is not available. Check the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-css/"
      }}>{`CSS reference`}</a>{` for a complete list of supported properties.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "css-preprocessors"
    }}>{`CSS Preprocessors`}</h3>
    <p>{`UXP only understands standard CSS. If you want to use preprocessors like `}<strong parentName="p">{`Sass`}</strong>{` or `}<strong parentName="p">{`SCSS`}</strong>{`, you must transpile them to CSS before bundling your plugin. This requires build tools like Webpack and a slightly different `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/tutorials/udt-deep-dive/plugin-workflows.md#working-with-bundlers"
      }}>{`debugging workflow`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "use-spectrum-for-consistency"
    }}>{`Use Spectrum for Consistency`}</h3>
    <p>{`We recommend using `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../fundamentals/user-interfaces/index.md#spectrum-web-components-swc"
      }}>{`Spectrum`}</a>{` for UI elements whenever possible. Spectrum is Adobe's design system and provides a consistent look and feel across all Adobe applications.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "reference-material"
    }}>{`Reference Material`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-css/index.md"

      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
